description: Dashboard Avançado - Página dedicada com métricas completas, gráficos, filtro por usuário e tabela de demandas

userStories.title: Remover toggle 'Habilitar tema personalizado' do Options
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to remove the 'Habilitar tema personalizado' toggle from the General settings section in options.html to simplify the interface, while keeping the light/dark/system theme toggle in the header working normally.
acceptanceCriteria: Remove the 'Habilitar tema personalizado' toggle switch (id: themeEnabledToggle) and its label/container from options.html
Remove the associated JS event listener and logic for themeEnabledToggle in options.js
The theme toggle button in the header (id: themeToggle) still works to switch between light/dark themes
ThemeManager and all theme CSS remain intact and functional
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Criar página scaffold do Dashboard dedicado
userStories.description: As a user, I want a dedicated dashboard page (separate from Options) so I have more space to visualize my demand data.
acceptanceCriteria: Create folder modules/dashboard/ with three files: dashboard.html, dashboard.js, dashboard.css
dashboard.html has a full HTML5 page that loads Bootstrap 5 CSS/JS, Bootstrap Icons, Chart.js, neuron-db.js, neuron-sync.js, theme-manager.js, neuron-utils.js, and its own dashboard.js/dashboard.css

HTML layout includes: header with title 'Neuron Dashboard' and navigation links (link to Options page), a section with id='stats-cards' for metric cards, a section with id='charts-section' for charts (2x2 grid placeholders), and a section with id='table-section' for the demandas table
dashboard.js initializes NeuronDB on DOMContentLoaded and calls ThemeManager to apply the saved theme
dashboard.css includes basic layout styles for the grid sections (responsive Bootstrap grid)
Register modules/dashboard/ folder in manifest.json under web_accessible_resources alongside existing entries
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: This is the foundational scaffold. All subsequent dashboard stories add components into the sections created here.

userStories.title: Cards de métricas principais no Dashboard
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see key metric cards at the top of the dashboard showing total demands, pending, short deadlines, overdue, completed, and completion rate at a glance.
acceptanceCriteria: Add 6 Bootstrap cards inside #stats-cards section: Total de Demandas (primary), Pendentes (warning), Prazos Curtos ≤2 dias (danger), Atrasadas/vencidas (danger), Concluídas (success), Taxa de Conclusão % (info)
Each card shows a Bootstrap Icon and a large number
Cards use Bootstrap grid: col-xl-2 col-md-4 col-sm-6 for responsive layout
dashboard.js calls NeuronDB.getStats() to populate all card values
Add a refreshDashboard() function in dashboard.js that re-fetches stats and updates all cards
Register NeuronSync listener for neuron_demandas and neuron_concluidas changes to call refreshDashboard()
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Uses NeuronDB.getStats() which already computes total, pendentes, prazosCurtos, atrasadas, concluidas, taxaConclusao.

userStories.title: Gráficos de distribuição de status e prazos
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a donut chart of demand status distribution and a horizontal bar chart of deadline distribution to quickly understand the composition of demands.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a donut chart (Chart.js) in #charts-section showing status distribution: Prorrogadas, Complementadas, Possível Respondida, Com Observação, Normal
Add a horizontal bar chart (Chart.js) in #charts-section showing deadline distribution: Atrasadas (< 0 days), Urgentes (0-2 days), Próximas (3-7 days), Normais (> 7 days)
Both charts read data from the stats object returned by NeuronDB.getStats()
Create a getThemeColors() helper function in dashboard.js that returns appropriate colors based on current theme (data-bs-theme attribute)
Charts resize responsively with the window (responsive: true, maintainAspectRatio: false in Chart.js options)
Charts update when refreshDashboard() is called
Listen for neuron-theme-change event to redraw charts with updated colors
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Stats object already has prorrogadas, complementadas, possivelRespondida, possivelobservacao, and byPrazoRange fields.

userStories.title: Gráfico de Top 10 Responsáveis
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a vertical bar chart showing the top 10 responsible parties by demand count to understand workload distribution.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a vertical bar chart (Chart.js) in #charts-section showing top 10 responsáveis by demand count
Chart reads data from stats.topResponsaveis array (already computed by NeuronDB.getStats())
Tooltip on hover shows full name and exact count
Chart uses theme-aware colors from getThemeColors()
Chart updates when refreshDashboard() is called
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: NeuronDB.getStats() already returns topResponsaveis as array of {name, count}.

userStories.title: Gráfico temporal de demandas por mês
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a line chart showing demands registered per month over the last 12 months to identify trends.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a line chart (Chart.js) in #charts-section showing demands registered per month for the last 12 months
X-axis shows months in MMM/YYYY format, Y-axis shows count
Group demands by month using the cadastroTimestamp field from each demanda
Add a second line showing demands completed (concluídas) per month using the concluidas set
Tooltip shows exact values on hover
Chart uses theme-aware colors and updates on theme change
Chart updates when refreshDashboard() is called
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Must compute monthly aggregation from demandas array and concluidas set. Use cadastroTimestamp (already computed by NeuronDB) for grouping.

userStories.title: Capturar nome do usuário logado via content script
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to capture the logged-in user's name from the Fala.BR page and store it in NeuronDB so the dashboard can filter demands by user.
acceptanceCriteria: In an existing content script that runs on Fala.BR pages, read the text content of element with id 'ConteudoForm_labelUsuario'
Store the captured username in NeuronDB metadata with key 'currentUser' using NeuronDB.setMetadata('currentUser', username)
Only update if the element exists and has non-empty text content
The capture runs once on page load (document_idle)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is a small change to an existing content script. The stored value will be used by the dashboard filter in US-008.

userStories.title: Filtro 'Minhas Demandas' vs 'Todas as Demandas' no Dashboard
userStories.description: As a user, I want to toggle between seeing only my demands and all demands so I can focus on what's relevant to me.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a Bootstrap button group toggle in the dashboard header area with two options: 'Minhas Demandas' and 'Todas as Demandas'
Read the current user name from NeuronDB.getMetadata('currentUser')
When 'Minhas Demandas' is selected, filter demandas to only those where responsaveis array includes the current user (case-insensitive comparison)
When 'Todas as Demandas' is selected, show all demandas without filtering
The filter selection persists via NeuronDB.setPreference('dashboardFilter', 'mine'|'all')
On page load, read saved preference and apply the filter
All stat cards, all 4 charts, and the demandas table (if it exists) update when the filter changes
Refactor refreshDashboard() to accept an optional filtered demandas array, or apply the filter before computing stats
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Depends on US-007 for the currentUser metadata. The filter must affect getStats()-equivalent calculations locally in dashboard.js since NeuronDB.getStats() doesn't support filtering.

userStories.title: Tabela de demandas com colunas e cores de urgência
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a complete table of all demands with urgency-based row coloring so I can quickly identify what needs attention.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a Bootstrap table inside #table-section with columns: Número (NUP), Data Cadastro, Prazo, Dias Restantes, Situação, Responsáveis
Populate the table from the filtered demandas array (respecting the Minhas/Todas filter from US-008)
Dias Restantes column shows a colored Bootstrap badge: badge bg-danger for < 0 days, badge bg-warning text-dark for 0-2 days, badge bg-success for > 2 days
Table rows have colored background based on urgency: table-danger class for overdue (< 0 days), table-warning class for urgent (0-2 days), no extra class for normal (> 2 days)
Responsáveis column shows names joined by comma
Table updates when refreshDashboard() is called
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Busca e ordenação na tabela de demandas
userStories.description: As a user, I want to search and sort the demands table so I can quickly find specific demands.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a search input field above the table with placeholder 'Buscar por número, situação ou responsável...'
Search filters table rows in real-time as the user types (case-insensitive match against numero, situacao, and responsaveis fields)
Each sortable column header (Número, Data Cadastro, Prazo, Dias Restantes) shows a clickable sort indicator
Clicking a column header sorts the table ascending; clicking again sorts descending
Active sort column shows arrow indicator (↑ for asc, ↓ for desc) using Bootstrap Icons bi-arrow-up / bi-arrow-down
Sort and search work together (search filters first, then sort applies to filtered results)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Sort by timestamps for date columns (prazoTimestamp, cadastroTimestamp), by numeric value for Dias Restantes.

userStories.title: Paginação da tabela de demandas
userStories.description: As a user, I want pagination on the demands table to navigate large datasets efficiently.
acceptanceCriteria: Add pagination controls below the table with Bootstrap pagination component: First, Previous, page numbers, Next, Last buttons
Add a select dropdown for items per page with options: 10, 25, 50, 100
Show text 'Mostrando X a Y de Z demandas' next to the pagination
Pagination resets to page 1 when the search query or filter (Minhas/Todas) changes
Items per page preference persists via NeuronDB.setPreference('dashboardItemsPerPage', value)
On page load, read saved items per page preference (default to 25)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Ações rápidas na tabela (Abrir no Fala.BR e Copiar NUP)
userStories.description: As a user, I want quick action buttons on each table row to open the demand in Fala.BR or copy the NUP number.
acceptanceCriteria: Add an 'Ações' column to the table as the last column
Each row has an 'Abrir no Fala.BR' icon button (bi-box-arrow-up-right) that opens the demand page in a new tab
The URL is constructed using the correct Fala.BR environment base URL from manifest.json host_permissions (default to production: falabr.cgu.gov.br)
Each row has a 'Copiar NUP' icon button (bi-clipboard) that copies the demand numero to clipboard using navigator.clipboard.writeText()
After copying, show a brief visual feedback: change icon to bi-clipboard-check and/or show a Bootstrap tooltip 'Copiado!' for 2 seconds
Buttons are compact using btn-sm btn-outline-secondary classes
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: For the Fala.BR URL, check the current environment or default to production.

userStories.title: Navegação: acesso ao Dashboard via Popup e Options
userStories.description: As a user, I want to easily access the dashboard from the extension popup and the Options page, and navigate back to Options from the dashboard.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a 'Dashboard' button in the popup (modules/popup/popup.html) that opens modules/dashboard/dashboard.html in a new tab using chrome.tabs.create()
Add a navigation link/button in the Options page header that opens the Dashboard page
The Dashboard page header already has a link to Options (added in US-002) - verify it works
Simplify or replace the existing 'Dashboard' tab content in Options with a card/link that says 'Abrir Dashboard completo' and opens the dedicated dashboard page
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: This touches popup.html, options.html, and verifies dashboard.html navigation. Keep the Options dashboard tab but replace its content with a redirect to the full dashboard.
